title: The Dark Knight Rises
logline: Eight years after taking the fall for Harvey Dent's crimes, a broken Bruce Wayne must rise from despair to save Gotham from the terrorist Bane, who plans to destroy the city with a nuclear bomb while holding its people hostage.
antagonistOrConflict: Bane and the League of Shadows' plan to destroy Gotham; Bruce's physical and psychological brokenness
tone: Epic, operatic, and somber with moments of hope; explores themes of pain, sacrifice, and redemption through large-scale spectacle and intimate character moments
themes: Rising from despair and physical/psychological defeat
The cost of heroism and sacrifice
Fear as both weapon and motivator

actBreaks.description: Batman is broken by Bane in the sewers and taken prisoner, ending his first attempt to stop the villain and launching the film into its darkest section.

validation.ruling: Bane breaks Batman's back with a horrific crack, removes his cowl as a trophy, and has his broken body carried away while Catwoman watches in shock—an irreversible defeat that fundamentally transforms the story from Batman's investigation into his imprisonment and Gotham's occupation, followed by a fade to black marking the act break.

actBreaks.description: Bruce escapes the prison pit and returns to Gotham, shifting from captivity and helplessness to active preparation for the final confrontation.

validation.ruling: Wayne successfully escapes the prison by climbing out of the hole into the desolate landscape, then pushes the rope into the hole for others and walks away—this completes his transformation and marks the irreversible shift from captivity to his return as Gotham's protector for the final act.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a three-act epic that follows a classic fall-and-rise trajectory, but with an unusually extended second act devoted to Bruce's imprisonment and Gotham's occupation. Act One (scenes 1-130, roughly 87 pages) establishes the broken status quo, introduces Bane's threat, and culminates in Batman's devastating defeat. Act Two (scenes 131-236, roughly 38 pages) is split between Bruce's physical and spiritual recovery in the prison and Gotham's descent into chaos under Bane's rule—a bold structural choice that keeps the protagonist offscreen for extended sequences while building tension through parallel storylines. Act Three (scenes 237-322, roughly 40 pages) delivers the climactic battle and resolution with remarkable efficiency, packing the uprising, reveals, and final confrontation into a propulsive final movement. The structure mirrors Bruce's journey: a long descent, a concentrated period of rebuilding, and an explosive return.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The opening 16 scenes are a bravura action prologue (the plane heist) that establishes Bane before cutting to Gotham eight years later. The first act moves methodically, intercutting Bruce's reclusive existence with Blake's investigation and Selina's schemes, building to the stock exchange raid (scenes 69-101) as a major set piece. The pace accelerates dramatically after Bruce's defeat (scene 130), with rapid cross-cutting between prison scenes and Gotham's occupation creating urgency despite Bruce's physical absence. The final act (starting scene 237) is relentless, with the climax sequence (scenes 260-303) comprising 44 rapid-fire scenes that intercut the street battle, the bomb chase, and multiple character arcs resolving simultaneously. The epilogue (scenes 304-322) provides emotional resolution across multiple locations and timelines.

incitingIncident.description: Blake tells Wayne that Gordon was shot chasing a masked man called Bane into the sewers and needs Batman's help, then reveals he knows Wayne is Batman because they met at an orphanage when Blake was a child.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. STUDY, WAYNE MANOR - MOMENTS LATER

validation.ruling: Blake's revelation to Wayne that Gordon needs Batman and his underground army discovery shatters Wayne's eight-year retirement status quo, forcing him to confront the threat he can no longer ignore and establishing the main conflict with Bane.

midpoint.description: The Mayor tells reporters that thousands of police heading into sewers is just a training exercise, then heads into the stadium with crowds to watch a football game.

validation.ruling: Scene #149 is merely setup (the Mayor dismissing concerns before entering the stadium). The true midpoint is scene #158 where the stadium field collapses into a massive sinkhole during the game, Bane emerges with the bomb, executes Dr. Pavel, and declares Gotham under the people's control—this is the irreversible point of no return that shifts the story from reactive investigation to proactive survival, raising stakes to citywide catastrophe.

climax.description: Bane prepares to execute Batman with a shotgun but Catwoman blasts Bane across the lobby with the Bat-Pod's cannon.

validation.ruling: Scene #281 is tactical planning dialogue, not the climactic confrontation. The true climax is scene #278 where Bane prepares to execute the defeated Batman with a shotgun but Catwoman blasts Bane with the Bat-Pod's cannon—this is the peak confrontation that directly resolves the antagonist threat and demonstrates Batman's arc of learning to trust others.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon delivers an emotional eulogy for Harvey Dent, declaring his belief in Dent before gathering his papers and fading to black.

scenes.heading: INT. LAND CRUISER JOSTLING OVER UNEVEN TERRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Three hooded men are guarded by militia inside a land cruiser traveling over rough terrain with a nervous bespectacled man.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRSTRIP, EASTERN EUROPE - DAY
scenes.summary: A CIA operative receives Dr. Pavel from militia at an airstrip, discovers the hooded men, and is told they are not Pavel's friends.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES OVER MOUNTAIN RANGE - DAY
scenes.summary: A commuter plane struggles over snow-capped mountains.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN CABIN, COMMUTER PLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The CIA man interrogates hooded prisoners by hanging them out the cargo door and firing his weapon, demanding to know who paid them and asking about Bane's mask.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES OVER MOUNTAIN RANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A massive transport plane looms dangerously close over the commuter plane.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN CABIN, COMMUTER PLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane reveals his plan to crash the plane with no survivors as the plane lurches and noise builds.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bane reveals his identity and deadly plan, initiating the hijacking sequence

scenes.heading: EXT. MOUNTAINS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Four men on tethers leap from the transport plane's ramp toward the commuter plane.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN CABIN, COMMUTER PLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane rises as armed men appear outside the windows and begin shooting.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES OVER MOUNTAIN RANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Armed men shoot through windows and attach grapples to the fuselage, lifting the tail of the commuter plane unnaturally.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, COMMUTER PLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Pilots battle the controls as the plane tilts forward.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN CABIN, COMMUTER PLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane breaks the CIA man's neck and drops him onto the sergeant as the cabin upends in chaos while Dr. Pavel remains strapped in his seat.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATLAS MOUNTAINS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Men climb the tail of the dangling commuter plane as its wings shear off.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN CABIN, COMMUTER PLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane breaks his handcuffs and somersaults down the cabin to stop beside Dr. Pavel.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES OVER MOUNTAIN RANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Armed men attach explosives to the tail and jump away before the tail explodes.

scenes.heading: INT. MAIN CABIN, COMMUTER PLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane transfers Dr. Pavel's blood into a body double via surgical tubing while armed men extract hooded prisoners on cables.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Mayor addresses a Harvey Dent Day celebration at Wayne Manor, praising the Dent Act's success against organized crime while a lone figure watches from a darkened balcony above.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHENS, WAYNE MANOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Maids gossip about Wayne never leaving the East Wing and being disfigured until Alfred enters and asks about Mrs. Bolton while preparing a tray.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, WAYNE MANOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: A maid walks through the empty house and unlocks the large oak door to the East Wing.

scenes.heading: INT. DRAWING ROOM, EAST WING, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The maid places a tray in the dark drawing room and notices the inner door is ajar.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Alfred tells Miranda Tate that Wayne refuses to see her, while Daggett mocks Wayne's reclusiveness and Alfred defends hosting the Dent Act event.

scenes.heading: INT. DRAWING ROOM, EAST WING, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bruce Wayne, gaunt and using a cane, lifts the lid of his dinner tray then freezes upon hearing something and limps into the next room.

scenes.heading: INT. SITTING ROOM, EAST WING, WAYNE MANOR, CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne fires an arrow into the target near the startled maid, then limps toward her as she nervously apologizes and mentions his lack of scars.

scenes.heading: EXT. DRIVE, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The maid removes her servant's disguise to reveal a black dress and pearls, then slips into a town car beside the Congressman.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST WING, WAYNE MANOR - LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne discovers the safe has been robbed and tells Alfred the maid took the pearls with their tracking device, dismissing Alfred's suggestion to raise an alarm.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP, MAJOR CRIMES UNIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake informs Gordon that Congressman Gilly is missing from the Wayne Foundation event, and Gordon reflects on the city's transformation while Blake questions why Gordon still acts like they're at war.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST WING, WAYNE MANOR - MORNING
scenes.summary: Alfred enters Wayne's bedroom with a tray to find the bed empty and receives no response when calling out.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDY, WAYNE MANOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Alfred hits three notes on the piano and the bookcase opens.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR SHAFT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Alfred descends into the caverns beneath Wayne Manor via the elevator shaft.

scenes.heading: INT. BATCAVE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne shows Alfred that the jewel thief wore fake fingerprints and identifies her as Selina Kyle, a cat burglar who has never been caught despite numerous close calls.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wayne returns to the Batcave and begins investigating, marking his re-engagement with the world

scenes.heading: EXT. WATER TREATMENT FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake and Ross investigate a teenage boy's body pulled from a water treatment basin, and Blake recognizes him as Jimmy from St. Swithin's boys home.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. SWITHIN’S HOME FOR BOYS - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake looks up at the shabby St. Swithin's Home for Boys building.

scenes.heading: INT. ST. SWITHIN’S HOME FOR BOYS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Father Reilly tells Blake that Jimmy aged out months ago because the Wayne Foundation stopped funding, and Blake asks to tell Jimmy's brother Mark himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAYGROUND, ST. SWITHIN’S - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Blake tells Mark about his brother's death and learns that boys who age out go into the tunnels where there is supposedly work available.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVE BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Selina delivers fingerprint transfers to Stryver, who signals thugs to lock the door and attack, but she shoots them and escapes as SWAT teams breach the bar.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Thugs open fire on SWAT teams with automatic weapons in the alley.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVE BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina finds the wounded Congressman under the bar, tells him to keep pressure on his leg, checks her appearance, and leaves.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIVE BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina feigns panic to Blake about a bleeding man inside, then slips away while Blake helps her onto a SWAT truck and enters the bar.

scenes.heading: INT. DIVE BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake enters the bar with his gun drawn and finds the wounded Congressman, radioing that he's okay.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon and SWAT teams pursue fleeing thugs into an alley, discover an open manhole, and Gordon descends into the sewers with three SWATs following.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon moves through the sewer tunnel with his flashlight as three SWATs fall in behind him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake arrives in the alley to learn Gordon took SWAT into the sewers, and Foley questions where the DWP guy is.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon hears gunfire ahead, the tunnel erupts in fire behind him blasting the SWATs, and he races forward through the tunnels.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake jumps back as a fireball bursts from the manhole.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon rounds a corner with his gun drawn and is struck from behind by a thug.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND BAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake argues with Foley about rescuing Gordon from the sewers but is ordered away, then gets an idea and heads for his patrol car.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Thugs drag the unconscious Gordon through sewer tunnels past work crews of muscular men with drills and armed mercenaries overseeing homeless street kids.

scenes.heading: INT. BANE’S LAIR, SEWERS - CONTINUOUS (NIGHT)
scenes.summary: Bane kills one thug for bringing Gordon to his lair, orders the other to search Gordon, reads Gordon's undelivered speech, and tells the thug he will kill him after the search is complete.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bane is revealed in Gotham's sewers with a major operation underway, and he obtains Gordon's speech revealing the truth about Harvey Dent

scenes.heading: EXT. WATER TREATMENT FACILITY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Blake pulls the barely alive Gordon from the raging waters at the catchment basin and hoists him up to safety.

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT HALL, WAYNE MANOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake tells Alfred he needs to see Bruce Wayne and threatens to get a warrant in the investigation of Harvey Dent's murder.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blake connects Wayne to the Dent case and forces a confrontation, bringing the outside world to Wayne's door

scenes.heading: INT. STUDY, WAYNE MANOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Blake tells Wayne that Gordon was shot chasing a masked man called Bane into the sewers and needs Batman's help, then reveals he knows Wayne is Batman because they met at an orphanage when Blake was a child.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blake directly confronts Wayne about being Batman and reveals Gordon needs him, pulling Wayne back toward his vigilante identity after years of retirement.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, WAYNE MANOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne asks Alfred about Bane, learning he's a mercenary who worked for Daggett in West Africa, then questions why the Wayne Foundation stopped funding boys' homes.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAYNE ENTERPRISES - DAY
scenes.summary: Miranda approaches Wayne Enterprises.

scenes.heading: INT. BOARDROOM, WAYNE ENTERPRISES - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Miranda tells Fox she wants to help Wayne Enterprises and warns that Daggett is acquiring shares, then Fox discovers Wayne waiting in his office and they discuss Wayne's financial losses from the failed energy project.

scenes.heading: INT. APPLIED SCIENCES - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Fox shows Wayne the Applied Sciences division where he's consolidated all Wayne Enterprises defense prototypes under one roof to keep them from falling into the wrong hands.

scenes.heading: INT. EXAMINATION ROOM, GOTHAM GENERAL HOSPITAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A doctor examines Wayne's X-rays and tells him his body is severely damaged with no cartilage in his knees, scarred kidneys, brain damage, and general scarring, recommending he take up drinking instead of physical activity.

scenes.heading: EXT. TENTH FLOOR, GOTHAM GENERAL HOSPITAL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne drops three floors down the exterior of the hospital.

scenes.heading: INT. PRIVATE ROOM, GOTHAM GENERAL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne in a ski mask visits Gordon's hospital room where Gordon tells him their victory was built on a lie and that evil is rising, insisting Batman must come back.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. LAMBORGHINI ON GOTHAM STREETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wayne parks outside shabby townhouses and watches a woman lead a man inside while checking a tracking device.

scenes.heading: INT. SELINA’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina hears a disturbance in the hallway involving Jen and a client.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL OUTSIDE SELINA’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina stops a man from hitting Jen by twisting his arm and forcing him to leave, then scolds Jen for trying to pickpocket violent clients.

scenes.heading: EXT. SELINA’S BUILDING, OLD TOWN, GOTHAM - LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne watches Selina exit her building and hail a cab, then follows using his tracker.

scenes.heading: EXT. MUSEUM, GOTHAM - LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne arrives at a museum charity event where paparazzi recognize him after years of hiding, and he uses a device to disable their cameras before entering.

scenes.heading: INT. MUSEUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne spots Selina wearing his mother's pearls at the charity ball, then meets Miranda who reveals she organized the event and challenges his cynicism about charity and his abandoned clean-energy project.

scenes.heading: EXT. MUSEUM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne discovers from the valet that someone claiming to be his wife said he was taking a cab home.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. LAMBORGHINI RACING DOWN STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina smiles while driving Wayne's stolen Lamborghini.

scenes.heading: INT. ROLLS ROYCE - LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne calls Fox from Alfred's car and tells him to prepare for unusual requests like in the past, signaling his return to action.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wayne commits to resuming his Batman activities by contacting Fox for equipment, marking his decision to come out of retirement.

scenes.heading: INT. BATCAVE - DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne tests a high-tech leg brace in the Batcave, enduring pain as it tightens, then successfully executes a roundhouse kick while Alfred expresses concern about his return to vigilantism.

scenes.heading: INT. STOCK EXCHANGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Traders discuss shorting Wayne Enterprises stock while a food delivery guy and construction workers gain access to the Stock Exchange building.

scenes.heading: EXT. STOCK EXCHANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and police arrive at the Stock Exchange but are blocked by construction vehicles, while Foley and the security chief argue about whether it's a hostage situation or robbery.

scenes.heading: INT. STOCK EXCHANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane learns the computer program needs eight more minutes and orders his team to go mobile.

scenes.heading: EXT. STOCK EXCHANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Six motorcycles explode out of the Stock Exchange and leap over barriers, escaping with hostages strapped to the backs of the bikes.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Police pursue the motorcycles but cannot shoot because the hostages are in the way.

scenes.heading: EXT. STOCK EXCHANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Foley orders his officers to back off because the robbers have hostages.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CRUISER ON GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A pursuing police cruiser's lights and engine die as darkness chases them through a tunnel, then Batman on the Bat-Pod roars past.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman makes his first public appearance in eight years, dramatically re-entering Gotham's conflict.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman uses an EMP to disable one motorcycle, then physically strikes the rider off his bike while racing past.

scenes.heading: EXT. STOCK EXCHANGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Foley rushes to Blake's cruiser announcing they've spotted Batman.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY INTERSECTION - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The remaining motorcycles split up at a highway intersection.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH OVERPASS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman fires an EMP rifle at the fleeing motorcycles from an overpass.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY OFF-RAMP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Two more motorcycles are disabled by the EMP and police move in to arrest the riders.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH OVERPASS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman sees the last two motorcycles disappear beneath an overpass and pursues on the Bat-Pod.

scenes.heading: INT. BLAKE’S CRUISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Foley orders all available police to pursue Batman, declaring he will take down Batman while Blake questions why they're not focusing on the armed robbers.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Police across Gotham mobilize cars, vans, and helicopters.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane transfers his hostage to the other motorcycle and peels off while Batman continues pursuing the bike carrying the laptop.

scenes.heading: INT. CHOPPER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A police spotter reports that one motorcycle without a hostage has separated from the chase.

scenes.heading: INT. BLAKE’S CRUISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Foley orders police to ignore the escaping robber and stay focused on capturing Batman, revealing he cares more about catching Batman than the actual criminals.

scenes.heading: INT. DAGGETT’S PENTHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Daggett and Stryver watch television coverage of Batman's return.

scenes.heading: INT. INNER OFFICE, DAGGETT’S PENTHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman opens Daggett's safe and finds it empty while watching the Batman pursuit on television.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane escapes into an outflow pipe while the entire police force pursues Batman.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman snatches the hostage from the motorcycle, crashes the bike, captures the rider, and discovers the completed computer program before police helicopters force him to flee.

scenes.heading: INT. DAGGETT’S PENTHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Daggett realizes Batman is drawing police away from Bane.

scenes.heading: INT. BLAKE’S CRUISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Foley angrily demands to know how police let Batman escape and orders them to close down downtown.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Thousands of police pursue Batman back into downtown Gotham.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman executes a ninety-degree turn into a blind alley where police trap him, but an unfamiliar engine sound suggests he has an escape plan.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon watches television coverage of Batman's return from his hospital bed and smiles.

scenes.heading: INT. DAGGETT’S PENTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stryver tells Daggett that Bane says the robbery was successful despite Batman's interference and that the captured men will die before talking.

scenes.heading: INT. INNER OFFICE, DAGGETT’S PENTHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman ambushes Daggett in his office, pins him to the wall with her heel, and demands to know where the Clean Slate program is.

scenes.heading: EXT. WINDOW-CLEANING PLATFORM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman escapes onto a window-cleaning platform and cuts the rope, plunging down the side of the building.

scenes.heading: EXT. FLAT ROOF - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Catwoman interrogates Daggett on a rooftop about the Clean Slate program, learning it doesn't exist, before Bane's trained killers surround them and Batman arrives in the Bat to rescue her.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP, SKYSCRAPER, GOTHAM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Batman confronts Catwoman on a rooftop, learning she sold Wayne's fingerprints to Daggett for the non-existent Clean Slate program, before she warns him about Bane and escapes.

scenes.heading: INT. BATCAVE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman crashes the Bat through the Batcave waterfall and argues with Alfred about his methods, refusing to give the police his technology while Alfred warns him about the dangers of his mission.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS (NIGHT)
scenes.summary: Alfred tells Wayne he won't bury him and threatens to leave, revealing he has something to tell Wayne about Rachel before she died, but Wayne insists Rachel intended to be with him.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, WAYNE MANOR - MORNING
scenes.summary: Wayne wakes to the doorbell ringing.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRCASE, WAYNE MANOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne moves through the empty house calling for Alfred, but Alfred is gone.

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT HALL, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox arrives and shows Wayne a newspaper revealing that fraudulent trades have bankrupted him and put Wayne Enterprises in Daggett's hands, though Fox assures him Applied Sciences is secure.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wayne loses his fortune and company, fundamentally changing his resources and position

scenes.heading: EXT. WAYNE ENTERPRISES RECYCLING PLANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Fox leads Miranda to a scrapyard recycling plant and they enter a Portakabin.

scenes.heading: INT. PORTAKABIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Portakabin floor descends, revealing it's an elevator taking Miranda down into a massive concrete tunnel.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR, FUSION REACTOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox and Wayne show Miranda the fusion reactor beneath the river, explaining it was built with her investment but doesn't work, and Wayne reveals he shut it down because it could be weaponized.

scenes.heading: INT. BOARDROOM, WAYNE ENTERPRISES - DAY
scenes.summary: At a Wayne Enterprises board meeting, Daggett attacks Wayne's financial losses, but Miranda reveals she bought his shares and now controls the company, appointing Fox as CEO.
scenes.turningPointNote: Miranda takes control of Wayne Enterprises, blocking Daggett and shifting corporate power

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, WAYNE ENTERPRISES - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne exits to an angry mob and reporters, discovers his Lamborghini is being towed, and Blake offers him a ride.

scenes.heading: INT. DAGGETT’S PENTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Daggett confronts Bane about the failed stock exchange plan and Miranda taking control of Wayne Enterprises, then Bane dismisses Stryver and places his hand on Daggett's shoulder.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CRUISER ON GOTHAM STREETS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Blake drives Wayne and asks why he wore a mask, and Wayne explains it was to protect people close to him and to be a symbol anyone could be, then asks Blake about Bane's whereabouts.

scenes.heading: EXT. SELINA’S BUILDING, OLD TOWN, GOTHAM - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake drops Wayne off at Selina's building, gives him money when Wayne admits he has none, then drives off when his radio squawks.

scenes.heading: INT. SELINA’S APARTMENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Selina packs a bag and hears Wayne's voice in the stairwell with Jen.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL OUTSIDE SELINA’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina tells Jen that Wayne isn't a mark and has no money.

scenes.heading: INT. SELINA’S APARTMENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne visits Selina's apartment and offers her the Clean Slate program in exchange for leading him to Bane, which she accepts.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake moves through a hospital corridor searching for someone.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake tells Gordon and Foley that Daggett's body was found in a dumpster and his name is on permits for tunnels under Gotham, but Foley dismisses the tunnel searches as a waste of resources.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAYNE MANOR - EVENING (END OF DAY)
scenes.summary: Wayne arrives at Wayne Manor in the rain without keys, and Miranda suggests they find a window to get inside.

scenes.heading: INT. GREAT ROOM, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS (END OF DAY)
scenes.summary: Wayne and Miranda enter through French windows, she tells him she's taken control of the company and will protect his parents' legacy, and he struggles to speak when she asks about a photo of Rachel.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME - LATER (NIGHT)
scenes.summary: Wayne and Miranda share an intimate moment by the fire as she reveals her impoverished childhood, and she suggests they leave Gotham together, but Wayne says he can't.

scenes.heading: INT. GREAT ROOM, WAYNE MANOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wayne watches Miranda sleep, then slips away.

scenes.heading: INT. BATCAVE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne enters the Batcave and picks up the cowl.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES ABOVE GOTHAM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Bat flies toward downtown Gotham, then Batman kills the lights and quietly autorotates into the canyons.

scenes.heading: INT. MIA TUNNEL - LATER
scenes.summary: Batman meets Catwoman in a tunnel and offers her the Clean Slate in exchange for taking him to Bane, and she agrees to lead him into a service tunnel.

scenes.heading: INT. SERVICE TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman leads Batman deeper into the tunnels, warning him that Bane's men are not average fighters.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWER TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman ambushes and defeats multiple mercenaries in the dark sewer tunnel, following Catwoman until a grate slams down separating them and lights come on, trapping him inside.

scenes.heading: INT. BANE’S LAIR, SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman reveals she betrayed Batman to save herself, Bane emerges and addresses him as Mr. Wayne, then brutally beats Batman in hand-to-hand combat, breaking through all his defenses and telling him his armor won't save him.

scenes.heading: INT. APPLIED SCIENCES - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mercenaries race through Applied Sciences equipment stacks.

scenes.heading: INT. BANE’S LAIR, SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane lifts the injured Batman high and breaks his back over his knee with a horrific crack, then removes his cowl as mercenaries carry Wayne's broken body away while Catwoman watches in shock.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman is defeated and his back is broken by Bane, removing him from Gotham and shifting the story into its darkest phase

scenes.heading: EXT. WAYNE MANOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake arrives at Wayne Manor in his cruiser and finds nothing through the windows.

scenes.heading: EXT. SELINA’S BUILDING, OLD TOWN, GOTHAM - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake spots Selina leaving her building with bags, recognizes her, and radios to tell Gordon he has a line on the Congressman's kidnapping before following her.

scenes.heading: INT. TERMINAL, GOTHAM INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: Selina moves through the airport terminal, spots a cop staring at her, and ducks into a service hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. SERVICE HALLWAY, AIRPORT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina punches a uniform cop through her hat brim, stuffs him in a closet, and places her mangled hat on his head.

scenes.heading: INT. JETWAY, AIRPORT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Selina walks down the jetway, spots security ahead, turns back, and finds Blake waiting with his badge.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake interrogates Selina in cuffs about the Congressman pressing charges, and she reveals she's running from Bane and warns Blake to be afraid of him.

scenes.heading: INT. STONE CELL - DAWN
scenes.summary: Wayne wakes in a filthy stone cell unable to move, and Bane tells him he's in an underground prison where he was born and will watch Gotham be destroyed on television before the city and his hope are extinguished.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wayne learns he's imprisoned in an inescapable pit and will be forced to watch Gotham's destruction, establishing the stakes of his imprisonment

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: A van carrying Selina pulls through the gates of Blackgate Prison.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Guards lead Selina through Blackgate Prison past catcalling inmates, and she breaks a huge inmate's arms without breaking stride when he reaches for her.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE ENTERPRISES - DAY
scenes.summary: Fox and Miranda arrive at Wayne Enterprises for a board meeting, with Fox questioning the need to brief the board on the energy project.

scenes.heading: INT. BOARDROOM, WAYNE ENTERPRISES - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox and Miranda enter the boardroom to find Bane holding the scared board hostage, and he takes Fox, Miranda, and volunteer board member Fredericks to where they buried their resources.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake tells Gordon he thinks Batman might not be coming back, then Foley bursts in reporting that the masked man kidnapped Wayne Enterprises board members and took three into the sewers, and Gordon orders every available cop sent down.

scenes.heading: INT. STONE CELL, UNDERGROUND PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne lies weak and feverish in his cell as a prisoner feeds him, and he watches another prisoner attempt and fail the rope climb to freedom, falling to his apparent death.

scenes.heading: INT. BANE’S LAIR, SEWERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Bane's men lead Fox, Miranda, and Fredericks through the debris-filled lair where mercenaries work on Fox's old inventions.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOCATIONS AROUND GOTHAM - EVENING
scenes.summary: SWAT teams and cops mass around sewer and tunnel entrances throughout Gotham.

scenes.heading: INT. MASSIVE TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane leads Fox and the others to where mercenaries plant charges on a wall with Dr. Pavel waiting, then the wall is blown open.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOCATIONS AROUND GOTHAM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A SWAT team hears a low echoing boom and heads into the sewers along with thousands of other police entering manholes and tunnel entrances across the city.

scenes.heading: INT. MASSIVE TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane forces Fox to activate the fusion reactor by threatening to kill Fredericks, and Miranda intervenes, placing her hand on the scanner and convincing Fox to do the same, turning the reactor on.
scenes.turningPointNote: The fusion reactor is activated, giving Bane access to a potential nuclear weapon and setting up the city's destruction

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Mayor tells reporters that thousands of police heading into sewers is just a training exercise, then heads into the stadium with crowds to watch a football game.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hundreds of SWAT officers wade through the sewers with flashlights, converging from different entry points toward the center.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CRUISER ON GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake reports to Gordon by phone about investigating Daggett's cement plants and pouring locations, then pulls up to another plant.

scenes.heading: INT. REACTOR PLANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Pavel tells Bane he has converted the reactor into a four-megaton nuclear bomb, and Bane orders the core removed despite Pavel's warning it will decay and detonate in five months.
scenes.turningPointNote: The reactor is weaponized into a nuclear bomb with a countdown, establishing the central threat of the film's second half

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMENT PLANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake questions a cement truck driver about blocking police at the stock exchange, realizes the worker behind him is reaching for a weapon, and spins to draw his gun.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CRUISER OUTSIDE CEMENT PLANT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Blake examines his map of cement pouring locations, realizes the pattern, and speeds away in his cruiser.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CRUISER ON GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake drives frantically while radioing dispatch to warn Foley that the police are heading into a trap.

scenes.heading: EXT. MTA TUNNEL - MOMENTS LATER (DAY)
scenes.summary: Foley receives Blake's warning that Bane has rigged explosives around the tunnels to trap the police underground and orders an immediate pullout.

scenes.heading: INT. BOILER ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane and mercenaries emerge from a hole in the boiler room floor.

scenes.heading: INT. GOTHAM STADIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Mayor watches the National Anthem from a corporate box at Gotham Stadium.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane listens to the football kickoff, says 'Let the games begin,' and the mercenaries detonate explosives.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWERS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The tunnel roof collapses behind the SWAT teams, trapping thousands of police officers throughout different tunnels.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STADIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The football field collapses into smoking ruins behind a running receiver, swallowing players, the Mayor's box explodes, and mercenaries pour out of the tunnel as the crowd screams.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bane's plan is executed: the stadium is destroyed, the Mayor killed, and Gotham's police force trapped underground in a coordinated attack that seizes control of the city

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CRUISER ON GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake's cruiser swerves to avoid erupting asphalt and is flipped onto its roof by an explosion.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM RIVER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: All bridges except the Gotham Bridge are destroyed by controlled demolitions.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane walks into the smoking stadium, takes a dead umpire's headset, and addresses the screaming crowd and TV cameras, telling Gotham to take control of their city.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. OVERTURNED CRUISER ON GOTHAM STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake crawls from his overturned cruiser and radios Foley, who reports every cop is trapped underground; Blake realizes not every cop is trapped and grabs his shotgun to flag down a car.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon's heart-rate monitor alarms and he wakes groggily in his hospital room.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL LOBBY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mercenaries with automatic weapons force their way into the hospital lobby and head for the stairwell.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CAR GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake drives through streets filled with dazed people, flames, and smoke billowing from the sewers.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM HOSPITAL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake races up to the hospital entrance.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, GOTHAM HOSPITAL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Two mercenaries move through the hospital corridor searching room to room.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon pulls himself out of bed, wheeling his IV.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, HOSPITAL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake jumps into the corridor with his gun raised as shots echo down the hall, then sprints toward them.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake enters a hospital room with his gun raised but freezes when Gordon presses a gun to his head from behind; Gordon reveals he has already killed the two mercenaries and tells Blake to get his coat.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STADIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mercenaries pull the glowing reactor core onto the stadium field as Bane announces it is the instrument of liberation.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTAGON WAR ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Pentagon analysts detect a radiation spike and confirm the device is nuclear.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STADIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane forces Dr. Pavel to his knees and orders him to identify himself to the world as a nuclear physicist.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTAGON WAR ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: CIA and military personnel scramble to verify Pavel's identity, noting he was confirmed dead in a plane crash but appears to be alive on camera.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES ABOVE GOTHAM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: F-22 fighter jets streak over Gotham River, assessing the island.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STADIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane has Dr. Pavel describe the neutron bomb to the world, then snaps Pavel's neck and announces the bomb is armed, mobile, and will detonate if anyone interferes from outside.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bane murders the only person who can disarm the bomb on live television, making the threat irreversible and Gotham completely isolated from outside help

scenes.heading: INT. PENTAGON WAR ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The General orders fighters pulled back, high-level reconnaissance begun, and requests the President on the line.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Mercenaries hold the Gotham Bridge as a Captain approaches to negotiate; the Lead Mercenary reveals the military will help keep hostages on the island because anyone who escapes could be the bomb's triggerman.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES ABOVE GOTHAM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: F-22s peel away from the burning city at nightfall as the President's voice announces they do not negotiate with terrorists but recognize realities.

scenes.heading: INT. STONE CELL, UNDERGROUND PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne watches his burning city on an old TV in his prison cell and starts to cry.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - CONTINUOUS (NIGHT)
scenes.summary: Blake drives through deserted streets while Gordon, listening to the President's speech, insists he must get in front of a camera as the symbol of law and order, but Blake warns Bane is using Gordon's speech about Harvey Dent.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Three camouflage Tumblers roll down a deserted Gotham street.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Bane emerges from a Tumbler in front of Blackgate Prison and addresses the gathered news crews, calling the prison a symbol of oppression.

scenes.heading: INT. CELLBLOCK, BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Prisoners including Selina strain to hear Bane's words about men languishing under the Dent Act.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane holds up a photo of Harvey Dent and describes him as a shining example of justice held over the people.

scenes.heading: INT. BLAKE’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon watches Bane's speech on TV while Blake gathers supplies and says they'll keep moving Gordon until they can get him in front of a camera.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane sets fire to Harvey Dent's picture and calls him a false idol meant to stop people from tearing down the corrupt city.

scenes.heading: INT. CELLBLOCK, BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Prisoners in Blackgate cheer and shout as Bane speaks about rebuilding the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane drops the burning picture and announces he will tell the truth about Harvey Dent in the words of Commissioner James Gordon.

scenes.heading: INT. BLAKE’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake's attention is caught by Bane's announcement as Gordon watches uneasily.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane reads Gordon's undelivered speech aloud, revealing that Harvey Dent became a psychopathic murderer who held Gordon's family at gunpoint and that Batman saved Gordon's son, not murdered Dent.
scenes.turningPointNote: The truth about Harvey Dent and the lie that built Gotham's justice system is exposed to the entire city, destroying the foundation of law and order

scenes.heading: INT. BLAKE’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake stares at the TV in shock while Gordon lowers his face into his hands as Bane reads his confession about praising the madman who tried to murder his own child.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane finishes reading Gordon's resignation speech to the stunned crowd, then asks if they accept his resignation, and individuals begin shouting yes.

scenes.heading: INT. CELLBLOCK, BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Prisoners cheer and pound against the bars as Selina watches.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane asks if the crowd accepts the resignation of all the liars and corrupt, and more of the crowd chants yes.

scenes.heading: INT. BLAKE’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake confronts Gordon about the lie, saying Gotham needs a hero now more than ever, but Gordon defends the lie as necessary and tells Blake he doesn't understand what it's like when the rules become shackles.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane orders a Tumbler to fire on the prison doors, declares Gotham belongs to the people to do as they please, and his men surge through the burning gates to free the prisoners.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bane releases all prisoners from Blackgate, completing his takeover by unleashing chaos and giving the city to the criminal element under the banner of liberation

scenes.heading: INT. BLACKGATE PRISON - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Selina moves through Blackgate Prison as prisoners stream out of open cells and mercenaries distribute weapons to the escaping inmates.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK BOULEVARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Mercenaries shoot open locks as people storm into expensive apartment buildings overlooking the park.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAUTIFUL OLD GOTHAM APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: People ransack a beautiful old Gotham apartment.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK BOULEVARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Mercenaries herd wealthy people onto the street.

scenes.heading: INT. STOCK EXCHANGE - DAY
scenes.summary: A kangaroo court presided over by a criminal from Blackgate is in session while Bane watches from the gallery.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEGANT APARTMENT, PARK BOULEVARD - DAY
scenes.summary: Tough-looking revellers crack open and spray champagne in an elegant apartment while Selina watches.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Hundreds of police live underground in the sewers, dividing up supplies lowered by ropes as Ross opens a pack.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STADIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mercenaries load the reactor core with its flickering countdown into the back of a black truck at Gotham Stadium.

scenes.heading: INT. STONE CELL, UNDERGROUND PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne attempts push-ups as the Prisoner helps him roll over, and the Blind Prisoner explains that Bane wears his mask to hold his pain at bay after a botched surgery, then tells the legend of a mercenary's child born in the pit.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET, DOWNTOWN GOTHAM - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake crouches behind a parked car on a snowy street, feeding a kite string down a storm drain while a Tumbler patrols nearby.

scenes.heading: INT. SEWER TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ross strains to reach and grasp a note dangling on a string in the sewer tunnel surrounded by dozens of cops living underground.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake winds the string, picks up a jerrycan, and hurries off.

scenes.heading: INT. ST. SWITHIN’S HOME FOR BOYS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake gives Father Reilly a jerrycan of gas for the bus and asks about the boys before leaving despite the priest's warning that cops are being hunted.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEGANT APARTMENT, PARK BOULEVARD - DAWN
scenes.summary: Selina finds a smashed family photograph in the ransacked apartment and tells Jen this isn't what she wanted when Jen says it's what Selina asked for.
scenes.turningPointNote: Selina's moral awakening — she realizes the chaos she helped create is not what she truly wanted, marking her shift toward redemption.

scenes.heading: INT. STONE CELL, UNDERGROUND PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: The Prisoner unties the rope from Wayne's chest and Wayne stands and walks.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME - LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne does push-ups while the Prisoner asks why he's building himself, and Wayne says he's not meant to die here as he focuses on his body.

scenes.heading: INT. STONE CELL, UNDERGROUND PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne wraps the rope around his chest and climbs toward the light as prisoners gather and bet, but he falls a hundred feet when his fingers brush the ledge, slamming into the rock face.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHECKPOINT, GOTHAM BRIDGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Mercenaries inspect supply trucks at a checkpoint on Gotham Bridge, checking boxes of rations before waving them through.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUPERMARKET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: An 18-wheeler pulls up to a supermarket where a line of Gothamites waits along the sidewalk.

scenes.heading: INT. 18-WHEELER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Three plain-clothes Special Forces men emerge from boxes inside the 18-wheeler, check and conceal their weapons, then exit.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPERMARKET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Special Forces men carry boxes into the store and the Store Manager leads them down into the lower storeroom.

scenes.heading: INT. LOWER STOREROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon and Blake meet Captain Jones and his Special Forces team, explaining that almost three thousand cops are trapped underground and have been getting supplies from Blake.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake and Captain Jones scout downtown when two Tumblers and a massive black truck pass by; Jones triggers a Geiger counter that confirms the truck contains the bomb before one Tumbler turns back and they flee down a side street.

scenes.heading: INT. LOWER STOREROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Captain Jones questions Gordon's plan while Blake insists Gordon tell him something, and Gordon begins to explain that things are more complicated.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET, DOWNTOWN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake leads them to the door of an empty bank.

scenes.heading: INT. TELLSONS BANK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: They cross the empty lobby to an elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICES ABOVE TELLSONS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Blake reveals that Wayne Enterprises executives are living in the offices above the bank, and Fox tells Captain Jones that Wayne Enterprises built the fusion reactor that Bane turned into a bomb, explaining that the core will decay and detonate in twenty-three days regardless of the trigger.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that the bomb will detonate automatically in 23 days regardless of the trigger fundamentally changes the stakes and urgency of the mission.

scenes.heading: INT. TELLSONS BANK - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Captain Jones and his men are ambushed by mercenaries with machine guns in the lobby as Blake dives back into the elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICES ABOVE TELLSONS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake grabs Miranda and flees toward the back stairs while mercenaries jump from the elevator firing, forcing Fox to freeze and lower himself to the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. TELLSONS BANK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane enters and places his hand over the barely-alive Captain Jones's nose and mouth until Jones is dead, then orders the mercenaries to give the people upstairs over for judgment and hang the bodies where the world will see.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bane murders Captain Jones in cold blood and orders public executions, demonstrating his absolute control and eliminating the Special Forces rescue attempt.

scenes.heading: INT. STONE CELL, UNDERGROUND PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne stares at the TV showing the Special Forces bodies hanging from Gotham Bridge and throws a stone into the screen.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME - LATER
scenes.summary: Wayne does endless push-ups, squats, and stretches while the Blind Prisoner tells him the leap to freedom is about the spirit and that fear is why he fails, but Wayne insists he's not afraid, he's angry.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDERGROUND PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne climbs the rock face again but a rock comes away in his hand and he drops, smashing into the rock face and hanging upside down as the Prisoner dismisses helping him down, and Wayne drifts into unconsciousness.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE PRISON - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wayne wakes to the Blind Prisoner telling him that not fearing death makes him weak, and Wayne responds that he fears dying in the prison while his city burns with no one to save it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wayne's realization that his true fear is failing Gotham, not death itself, represents his psychological breakthrough needed to escape the pit.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE PRISON - MORNING
scenes.summary: Wayne refuses the rope and climbs without it as prisoners gather and chant; he reaches the precipice where bats explode from the cliff face, then makes the leap successfully and climbs out of the hole.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wayne successfully escapes the pit without the rope, overcoming his physical and psychological prison and returning to save Gotham.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAVE PRISON, NEAR A FORTRESS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne climbs out of the hole into desolation, shoulders his pack, pushes a coil of rope into the hole for other prisoners, and walks away.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT, ABANDONED STOCK EXCHANGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Fox watches from among huddled professionals in the basement as mercenaries drag Stryver upstairs yelling that there's been a mistake and demanding to see Bane.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED STOCK EXCHANGE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Dr. Jonathan Crane presides over Stryver's kangaroo court trial, sentencing him to death by exile despite Stryver's protests that he's one of them, while Bane watches silently.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM RIVER - DAY
scenes.summary: A Blackgate inmate uncuffs Stryver and whispers to follow the thick ice, then Stryver shuffles onto the ice and the river swallows him a hundred yards out.

scenes.heading: INT. EMPTY OFFICE BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon waits with a handful of cops for reinforcements, and when Blake arrives with only ten cops, Gordon realizes they have eighteen hours before the bomb goes off and need to mark the truck with GPS.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOLEY’S BROWNSTONE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon confronts Foley at his home for hiding while the city is under occupation, and Foley argues that moving against Bane will trigger the bomb, but Gordon insists there is no trigger and reveals the bomb will go off anyway, convincing Foley to rejoin the fight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Foley is convinced to rejoin the resistance after learning the bomb will detonate regardless, bringing crucial manpower back to Gordon's side.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Selina stops two gangbangers from beating a kid who stole an apple, takes one bite as tax and returns it to him, then Wayne appears behind her and they discuss her role in Bane's plan before she agrees to take him to Bane.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon and Miranda work in two-man teams to track the bomb truck; Miranda uses a Geiger counter while Gordon's men slow the truck, and when the needle hits two hundred, Miranda signals Gordon who marks the truck with a GPS tracker.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT, ABANDONED STOCK EXCHANGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Thugs bring a hooded prisoner into the basement and Fox removes the hood to reveal Bruce Wayne, who nods silently as Miranda recognizes him.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLONNADE, HOLDING AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman takes out two thugs with a heel and a punch, unlocking Fox's wrists before disappearing.

scenes.heading: INT. DARK SPACE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wayne and Fox discuss blocking the triggerman's signal in the Bat-Bunker, and Fox says he needs the EMP cannon-guidance mount from the Bat while Wayne retrieves equipment and the Batsuit rises from the floor.
scenes.turningPointNote: Wayne reclaims the Batsuit and plans to block the trigger signal, marking Batman's return and the beginning of the final confrontation.

scenes.heading: INT. ABANDONED STOCK EXCHANGE - EVENING (LATE DAY)
scenes.summary: Crane presides over Gordon's trial for espionage and sabotage, and when Gordon chooses death over exile, Crane sentences him and his men to exile anyway.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL, SKYSCRAPER - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Wayne bounds up the stairwell with Fox following out of breath.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP, SKYSCRAPER, GOTHAM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Fox emerges on the rooftop to see Wayne uncovering the Bat and begins pulling apart the gun mount as the sun sets.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM RIVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon leads his men onto the creaking ice, finds a pool of liquid and a flare, then hears Batman's voice telling him to light it up; Gordon lights the flare and sends a trail of fire across the ice that forms the shape of a bat on a building.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman's dramatic return with the flaming bat symbol signals hope to Gordon and the trapped police, marking the beginning of the final battle for Gotham.

scenes.heading: INT. FOLEY’S BROWNSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Foley's kids call him to a window where he sees the flaming bat signal.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A nervous mercenary shows Bane the bat signal; Bane declares Batman's return impossible.

scenes.heading: INT. FOLEY’S BROWNSTONE, BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Foley pulls his dress blues from their hiding place under the floorboards.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTFLOW PIPE, ACKERMAN PARK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake takes out mercenaries guarding the outflow pipe entrance.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM RIVER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman gives Gordon a metal box that blocks the bomb's remote detonator signal and tells him to get it onto the truck by sunrise.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTFLOW PIPE, ACKERMAN PARK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake rescues Ross but Ross is shot; Batman saves Blake from execution and tells him to wear a mask to protect people he cares about.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon checks the metal box, hides it under his coat, and signals his men.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman shows Catwoman the Bat-Pod and instructs her to clear the midtown tunnel; she starts it and says she'll escape the blast radius instead of helping.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS LEADING TO SOUTH TUNNEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Catwoman arrives at the blocked tunnel entrance on the Bat-Pod.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREETS - DAWN
scenes.summary: Foley leads the line of cops forward toward Bane's mercenaries and two Tumblers at City Hall despite threats to disperse.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane orders his forces to open fire on the advancing cops.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET, DOWNTOWN GOTHAM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Bat destroys both Tumblers and the cops charge into hand-to-hand combat with the mercenaries.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final battle for Gotham begins as Batman returns and the cops engage Bane's forces in all-out war.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS LEADING TO SOUTH TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman hears the distant battle and blasts open the tunnel blockage, clearing her escape path.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon hears the fighting and nervously checks the signal-blocking box.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane and Batman face each other in the battle; Bane leaps at Batman and they exchange powerful blows while Miranda watches from City Hall.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon's team stops the black truck but discovers it's empty—the wrong truck.

scenes.heading: INT. REACTOR PLANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox hurries into the reactor and starts throwing switches.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. SWITHIN’S HOME FOR BOYS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake sends the boys to warn people to evacuate through the South Street tunnel or bridge, then get back to the bus.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A Tumbler separates Batman and Bane mid-fight; Batman chases after it.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman smashes Bane through the door and rips the tubes on Bane's mask, causing him agony; Batman searches Bane for the trigger but Bane reveals he doesn't have it.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIFTH STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon jumps into the second black truck and hangs onto the bumper, struggling to activate the signal-blocking box.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Miranda stabs Batman in the side and reveals she is Talia, daughter of Ra's al Ghul; she explains how Bane protected her escape from the pit and how she will complete her father's work.
scenes.turningPointNote: The true villain is revealed as Miranda is exposed as Talia al Ghul, who has orchestrated everything and now has Batman at her mercy.

scenes.heading: INT. TEMPLE, LEAGUE OF SHADOWS - DAY
scenes.summary: Bane trains in a prototype mask while Ra's al Ghul watches and turns away, unable to accept him.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Talia tells Batman that Bane was excommunicated for loving her and that she honors her father by finishing his work; she activates the detonator.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIFTH STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The light on Gordon's metal box blinks as he hangs onto the truck.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Talia learns the truck is under attack and realizes Gordon blocked her signal; she orders a convoy to secure the bomb for eleven minutes until detonation.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Talia's convoy of three Tumblers blasts through the streets; Foley fires at them but is smashed aside by Talia's Tumbler.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bane prepares to execute Batman with a shotgun but Catwoman blasts Bane across the lobby with the Bat-Pod's cannon.
scenes.turningPointNote: Catwoman saves Batman's life at the last second, choosing to return and help rather than escape.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake tries to evacuate the boys across the bridge but the Uniform refuses and threatens to blow the bridge if anyone crosses.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Talia's convoy of Tumblers surrounds and protects the black truck carrying the bomb.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CITY HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman tells Catwoman they need to force the convoy east to the reactor entrance.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Catwoman roars down the City Hall steps on the Bat-Pod.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK TRUCK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon climbs into the truck and sees the bomb heating up with nine minutes on the countdown.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake walks toward the bridge despite warning shots at his feet.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman and Catwoman engage the convoy in a rolling battle, trying to force it east while Catwoman dodges heavy fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Uniform orders the bridge blown; it explodes in a massive fireball as Blake dives backward.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman and Catwoman destroy two Tumblers as the countdown reaches seven minutes.

scenes.heading: INT. REACTOR PLANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox fires up the reactor but notices something is wrong.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake sees the destroyed bridge and curses the officers for killing everyone.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK TRUCK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon fires blindly through the bulkhead from inside the truck trailer, trying to hit the driver.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB, BLACK TRUCK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon's bullets kill the driver; Talia takes the wheel as bullets continue flying through the cab.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake tells Father Reilly to get the boys on the bus despite having nowhere to go.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Bat forces the black truck into a hard right turn.

scenes.heading: INT. TRUCK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Talia realizes Batman is pushing them toward the reactor entrance to reconnect the core and smiles knowingly.

scenes.heading: INT. REACTOR PLANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Water smashes into the reactor from both directions, flooding it as Fox climbs a ladder.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Catwoman's blasts flip a Tumbler into the black truck's path, causing both vehicles to crash into the tunnel on their sides.

scenes.heading: INT. REACTOR PLANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox braces on the ladder as a wave smashes painfully into him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TUNNEL ENTRANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon exposes the glowing core but cannot grab it; dying Talia reveals she flooded the reactor and there's no way to stop the bomb.
scenes.turningPointNote: Talia's final revelation that the reactor is flooded eliminates the plan to reconnect the core, leaving only one desperate option.

scenes.heading: INT. REACTOR PLANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox pulls himself above the rising whitewater in the flooded reactor.

scenes.heading: EXT. TUNNEL ENTRANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Talia dies; Batman attaches a hoist to the core and tells Catwoman he'll fly the bomb out over the bay with no autopilot, meaning he cannot bail out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman commits to a suicide mission to save Gotham, revealing there's no autopilot and he cannot escape.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake shoves children onto a bus for protection from the blast while Father Reilly protests that it's an atom bomb, and Blake furiously insists he won't let the children die without hope.

scenes.heading: EXT. TUNNEL ENTRANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon tells Batman he never cared who he was, and Batman responds that a hero can be anyone, reminding Gordon of the simple act of putting a coat around a boy's shoulders as the Bat fires up.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM BRIDGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake shouts for the children to get down as an explosion rocks downtown Gotham, but a little boy points out the window at the Bat thundering out of the city trailing smoke with the core hanging from it.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARDENS, WAYNE MANOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon reads from A Tale of Two Cities at Bruce Wayne's grave while Fox, Blake, and a tearful Alfred stand opposite, and Fox places a gentle hand on Alfred.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bruce Wayne's funeral marks the apparent death of the protagonist and the emotional climax of his sacrifice.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT ENTRANCE, WAYNE MANOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake tells Gordon he's quitting the force because he can't take the injustice of no one knowing who saved the city, and Gordon responds that they know it was Batman.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAZA, DOWNTOWN GOTHAM - DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon watches from a platform as a statue of Batman is unveiled in downtown Gotham.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWYER’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne's lawyer addresses a small group about disposing of considerable assets from the estate as Blake slips into the room.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAYNE MANOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Movers bring artworks and furniture out of Wayne Manor while Alfred watches as a solitary figure.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWYER’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The lawyer continues explaining the will as Blake shifts uncomfortably, out of place in the office.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAYNE MANOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Movers carry bunk beds into Wayne Manor as Father Reilly shepherds wide-eyed children from a schoolbus toward the house, now designated as The Martha and Thomas Wayne Home for Children.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWYER’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake receives a sports bag from the clerk after giving his legal name, and she tells him she likes the name Robin as he smiles and leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. APPLIED SCIENCES - DAY
scenes.summary: Fox learns from techs that the autopilot system was fixed six months ago by Bruce Wayne, and an idea starts to form as he turns away from the rotor.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fox discovers Bruce may have survived, fundamentally recontextualizing the funeral and Batman's sacrifice.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake sits in his car examining a GPS device and a sports bag containing coordinates, climbing rope, and shackles, then gets out and follows the GPS into the trees.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAFÉ, FLORENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Alfred sits at his usual table at a café in Florence and orders a Fernet Branca.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake finds a large waterfall, checks his GPS confused, stares at the waterfall, and reaches for climbing gear.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCU - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon steps onto the MCU roof with his pile of arrest reports.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAFÉ, FLORENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Alfred glances up at a couple of strangers at the next table while sipping his drink, then looks back down.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake bursts through the waterfall from inside the cave, landing painfully on shale, then gets to his feet and moves cautiously into the cave.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCU - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon notices the searchlight has been fixed with a brand new steel Bat symbol and looks up into the night.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake lights his torch in the cave and ducks as thousands of screeching bats descend around him.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAFÉ, FLORENCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Alfred sees Bruce alive across the café tables with Selina, Bruce smiles at Alfred who nods, and Alfred pays his bill content.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bruce is revealed to be alive, fulfilling Alfred's dream and completing his arc from broken to redeemed and at peace.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - DAY
scenes.summary: Blake rises and is swallowed by the darkness of bat wings in the cave.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blake's symbolic rebirth in the Batcave suggests the mantle of Batman will continue, completing the film's theme that a hero can be anyone.
